title: Agape and 4E Cognitive Science
essence: Integrating perennial problem responses with worldview attunement, Vervaeke addresses the interaction between historical forces and persistent vulnerabilities. Following Wolf on meaning as connectedness, he identifies the core tension: the loss of three orders undermines objective attractiveness. Rather than recovering objective value, Vervaeke proposes agape—caring for the conditions of meaning-making itself—as transjective and transcendental without being supernatural. He then demonstrates how 4E cognitive science (embodiment, embeddedness, enactment, extension) recovers the three orders: embodiment and embeddedness restore nomological continuity; emergence and complexification restore normative order; and open-ended optimization replace narrative teleology. Integrating deep continuity hypothesis, autopoiesis, emotion as salience-sculpting, and positive psychology's excellence, Vervaeke reconstructs a scientific worldview that legitimates sacredness, meaning-making, and self-transcendence.
keyClaims: Agape—caring for conditions of meaning-making itself—is transjective and transcendental without metaphysical supernatural claims.
4E cognitive science recovers nomological order through embodiment-bioeconomy-niche construction; normative order through emergence and complexification.
Emotions sculpt salience landscapes and are intrinsic to relevance realization; without emotion, cognitive agents face combinatorial explosion.
Consciousness functions to set decision criteria for perception; perspectival knowing drives optimal criterion-setting.
Positive psychology studies excellence and wisdom; transcendence is movement into depth of nature and psyche, not above-ness.

concepts.name: agape
concepts.summary: Love for its own sake; caring for conditions of meaning-making as intrinsically valuable and transjective.

concepts.name: subjective attraction
concepts.summary: Personal draw toward something; requires meeting objective attractiveness for meaning in a transjective relationship.

concepts.name: objective attractiveness
concepts.summary: Value independent of personal valuing; undermined by scientific worldview but replaced by transjective conditions.

concepts.name: embodiment
concepts.summary: Mind arises through immediate coping with world; deep continuity between abstract cognition and sensory-motor action.

concepts.name: embeddedness
concepts.summary: Agent embedded in environment; transjective coupling through niche construction and evolutionary dynamics.

concepts.name: enactive
concepts.summary: Cognition as inherently enacted; mind emerges through coupled agent-environment interaction.

concepts.name: extended mind
concepts.summary: Cognition extends beyond brain through psychotechnologies, tools, and distributed social processes.

concepts.name: emergence
concepts.summary: System properties emerge from organization that component parts lack; mind emerges from brain-body-world coupling.

concepts.name: autopoietic
concepts.summary: Self-making systems that self-organize to protect and promote agency; unlike tornadoes, systems seek viable conditions.

concepts.name: niche construction
concepts.summary: Organism shapes environment while environment shapes organism; reciprocal evolutionary coupling.

concepts.name: salience landscaping
concepts.summary: Emotions sculpt what stands out; emotions make relevance realization apparent in agent-arena relationship.

concepts.name: wisdom
concepts.summary: Excellence in cognitive coping-caring; systematic capacity to address self-deception and respond to perennial problems.

concepts.name: transcendence into
concepts.summary: Movement into depths of nature and psyche rather than transcendence above; integrated with reciprocal realization.

concepts.name: open-ended optimization
concepts.summary: Continual improvement without fixed telos; replaces narrative order with post-narrative depth-seeking.

people.name: Susan Wolf
people.summary: Meaning in life as connectedness; identifies crisis of objective attractiveness in scientific worldview.

people.name: Francisco Varela
people.summary: Third-generation 4E cognitive science founder; deep resonance between neuroscience and Buddhist dharma.

people.name: Evan Thompson
people.summary: Deep continuity hypothesis; mind-in-life framework; development of 4E cognitive science.

people.name: Dennis Walsh
people.summary: Philosophy of biology; niche construction and extended evolutionary synthesis.

people.name: Antonio Damasio
people.summary: Descartes' Error; emotions essential to cognition; without emotion, incapacitation despite intact calculation.

people.name: Ursula Goodenough
people.summary: Sacred Depths of Nature; transcendence into nature; recovering sacredness in scientific worldview.

people.name: Mark Rowlands
people.summary: New Science of Mind; extended mind and embodied phenomenology convergence.

people.name: Robert Sternberg
people.summary: Wisdom in psychology; pioneering cognitive scientific study of wisdom.

people.name: Lau (neuroscientist)
people.summary: Consciousness functions to set decision criteria for perception based on situational awareness.

crossRefs.context: Previous episode presented psychotechnologies; this situates them in naturalized worldview.
Next episode addresses religion-not-religion and wisdom as meta-virtue coordinating all responses.
Earlier neural implementation of RR; consciousness tied to salience landscaping and relevance.

quotableMoments.quote: Agape is to love for its own sake. The process of meaning making is the process of being a person ultimately. This is agape.
quotableMoments.context: Defining agape as caring for conditions of meaning-making itself.

quotableMoments.quote: Mind is not programmed software or rule-bound manipulation of symbols. Instead, the mind arises through immediate coping with the world.
quotableMoments.context: Varela on embodiment; rejecting cognitivist abstraction in favor of participatory engagement.

quotableMoments.quote: Emotions are the way in which relevance realization is brought up into the level of your salience landscaping.
quotableMoments.context: Emotion's role in making RR apparent through agent-arena relationship sculpting.

quotableMoments.quote: Transcendence into the depths of nature. Transcendence into the depths of the psyche.
quotableMoments.context: Post-supernatural transcendence as movement into complexity rather than above-ness.
